name: Poppy-Seed Cake with Lemon Glaze
recipeIngredient: Whites of 5 large eggs at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Spray the interior of a 10-inch bundt pan once lightly with the vegetable oil spray.
2. Puree the apricot halves to a smooth consistency in a food processor or blender. Stir together the flour, poppy seeds, baking powder, and salt until well combined.
3. Transfer the apricot puree to a large mixing bowl. Beat the egg whites at medium-high speed until thick. Gradually add the remaining 1/2 cup of granulated sugar, beating until stiff peaks form and the meringue looks glossy.
4. Stir 1/3 of the meringue mixture into the batter to lighten it, then fold in the remainder. Pour batter into the bundt pan. Bake for about 25 minutes, or until a tester comes out clean and the center of the cake springs back to touch.
5. Transfer cake to a rack and cool for 30 to 40 minutes. In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice and confectioner's sugar for glaze. Drizzle glaze over the cooled cake. Garnish each serving with sliced apricots if desired.
[0.4 gram of fat/serving] from "Just Desserts: secret to fat-free-eating lies in substitution" Chicago Tribune Magazine, April 25, 1993.
